contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 180
- Description: Discussion on how libraries have changed from just offering books to be more experience focused and every library publishes the events they hold
  
  This is a great place for community to gather.
  
  Libraries pay for experience to be made available to the community if they like the experience
  
  Book selling is a dying trade since there are more mediums now competing to fulfill the same function of reading.
  
  Also the importance of identifying the why and not mistaking it for the medium utilized to deliver the WHY.
  
  A physical book is the medium. The WHY might be to educate, to offer an experience, to express beauty
  
  Knowing the WHY allows us to be flexible about the HOW.
  
  Also Amazon.com when it took over the books veritical really disrupted that market sector. And forced all the book stores, libraries to shift their purpose away from just functional to experiential. 
  
  With the pandemic and the now with AI, people's desire for something unique and experiential has increased and with AI, perhaps what used to be costly to deliver has dropped which means more unique experiences can start flourishing
  
  Also the door to door sales man which used to sell books and how it was happening back during the days of the silk road which was considered none viable is starting to become something that people desire again.... In essence we have reinvented something that is really old.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh, Philip Lee - husband of june, June Jo
- TDG Issued: 300.00
- Attached Filename: None
- Destination Contribution File Location: No file attached
